title: How ROTC Cadets Eliminated the Old Dominion Shooter in 4 Minutes
body:
This guy was a refugee who was convicted of funding a terrorist organization in 2016. He was also plotting attacks, buying an AR after searching what weapon another terrorist used in an attack on other US servicemen. He then spent 7 years in prison, and then committed this heinous attack 6 months after release.

Why was he still allowed to be in this country??? That’s disgusting.
author: @Reginald_Harrison

title: How ROTC Cadets Eliminated the Old Dominion Shooter in 4 Minutes
body: I cannot express how much better I feel today knowing there are young men still existing in  America who are patriots. Humble, intelligent, brave. The media ignoring their story is loud. I hope this goes viral.
author: @DallasJonez

title: How ROTC Cadets Eliminated the Old Dominion Shooter in 4 Minutes
body:
What I love about this interview is, not only is the Cadet being allowed to tell his story and that of his fellow bad ass Cadets, but is seems every question and comment Shawn makes is worded to help this young man with his current head space. Every he says is leading him to a positive answer, almost.
Shawn shows mad respect to those who stood up to eliminate the threat. Really hope, after processing all that happened, this guy understands that he did something heroic.

title: HOW MUCH DOES OLD DOMINION PAY CLASS A DRIVERS?
body:
I work for OD. I came on over after hauling construction equipment for almost 2 years. I can confirm OD is a good company. I was hired on as a Combo driver (P&D priority, linehaul cover driver).

I am at a small terminal, start time is 9 a.m and usually work 8-10 hours a day, some of our routes branch out further so they might work a little more hours. Everyone on the P&D side help each other so it works out great. Home everyday, weekends off (optional linehaul runs available), 2 weeks vacation first year. OT>50 hours. The only downside is at a smaller terminal you might be waiting a while for a linehaul route because OD has such a low turnover rate.

I will say that LTL isnt for everyone. I had never done it when i came over and it stressed me the hell out for a month. You dont know where anything is and there are alot of places you go that a 48ft tractor trailer shouldn't be plus backing off busy streets, residential deliveries can be a pain in the ass etc.. However, once you get used to it gets alot better. Every day is a mystery, you never go the same places every single day.
author: @josericardo5994

title: HOW MUCH DOES OLD DOMINION PAY CLASS A DRIVERS?
body:
I talked to an OD drivers three years ago and he said the top pay was a little over $36 and takes three years to get there.

But like many businesses I’ve seen in my life the pay might get lower instead of higher.

title: Old Dominion - One Man Band (Official Video)
body:
I have been married to the love of my life for 34 years. She has been fighting stage 4 breast cancer for 5 years now. The cancer is back and has now spread to her bones. I sing this song to her and tell her to fight because I don't want to be a one-man band. She is doing her best!

Update - we met with her Oncologist yesterday. She loves going to the gym to workout. Doctor advised no more workouts. Cancer is creating fracture in the spine. She does not have pain. We are praying radiation and new drugs will slow the spread so we can enjoy a little more time together. She is one of the nicest people you will ever meet. My advice...thank God for everyday you have together because one day it will be your last, never go to bed mad at each other, hold her close and dance with her everyday.
author: @ronnvandusen7477

title: Old Dominion - Written in the Sand
body: I must say that Im a 34 year old black woman, when I was 9 , my mom met a white guy who helped her raise me and my two other siblings as well as a child he had of his own, who was 1 or 2 months old. Anyway, he would play country music, Shamus twain, Leann rimes, billy Cyrus, just to name a few and me and my siblings we would laugh in the back seat , remind you we were just kids, when I got around 15 I started to love Shamus twain, but wouldn't admit it, I also noticed that I had a singing talent that I was too shy to reveal to anyone. When I was about 18 I kind of sort of didnt listen to country, not that I didn't like it anymore, but everywhere I went there was some new rap song or video, new styles and I just wanted to try didn't things, only to realize by the age of 25 those songs and dances had no meaning to life, and didn't suit me and my needs no longer.Country music in my opinion is more positive, has a feel good feeling every time, makes you think about what's most important and valuable in your own life, and so much more. 5years ago I met a black guy, who was a country/hood guy, which means he would change his personality or accent between a country guy and a hood guy, but one thing that stayed the same was his clothes, he had cow boy boots, blue jeans, his cowboy hat and and dip in his pocket. He got picked on at work but I was the only black person who understood. I don't judge anyone and what they prefer in their lives, just be happy with you and what makes you happy, if no one understands that, then it's their own stupidity, and you should feel sorry first-generation for trying to please everyone, but themselves.
author: @ebonyeyed08ti

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: I just cannot imagine how long distance truckers used to get around before GPS.  Just tons and tons of old fashioned maps, with no clue if there's road construction, accidents, or if the road is closed, or if the road/street is no longer there.  Also, with so many new subdivisions coming up, new roads don't even appear on paper maps.  Insane when you think about it now.
author: @user-sg8kq7ii3y

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: Retired UK truck driver here, when you have some experience under your belt apply for a job in the rock and roll industry. I did 13 years and went on tour with the best of the best including Dave Matthews, Eagles, Lenny Kravitz, Genesis and so many more. To me it wasn't a job it was a beautiful lifestyle. You will love it xx

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: If you're reading this right now, I just want to say; I'm rooting for you. We’re strangers, but that doesn’t mean I don’t care. I know what it’s like to feel like you're pushing a boulder uphill. I felt that way until I picked up Manifest and Receive by Eva Hartley I can’t explain it perfectly, but it changed my whole perspective. After checking it out on youtube and reading it things have been flowing—money, peace, confidence. maybe this is your moment too. Stay open. Wishing you more than you ever imagined✨

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: You really need a GPS for trucks. Your phones GPS will not keep you off roads restricted for your truck and trailer, and will not warn you of low bridges.  Plus truck GPS will show you truck stops. You should get a Garmin. Good luck and keep it dirty side down.

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: Seeing a 21-year-old handling a massive rig is the definition of pure grit. I love the comment about feeling like 'kids playing adult'—it hits so close to home. I write and produce coming-of-age country music specifically about that exact feeling of young people taking on the world before they're fully ready. You're holding it down perfectly. Stay safe out there!

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: that feeling you get after gong through the sketchiest lines of road and then you see a " x Highway turn here ", Specially Driving in NYC, I held my breath plenty of timess
author: @NotShefu

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body:
Well done. Made the right decision not going down under the bridge you were uncomfortable with. I can tell you, that in PA there are many bridges like that, where the road dips down and it looks like you will never make it, but you do. Signs are posted, if you for some crazy reason don't, it would not be you at fault.
However, right decision, because if you are unsure or feel it is not right, then it is good to know how to get yourself through it going another way. Nice work
author: @koolerstream921

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: Always back against the pin before going forward. You’ll save yourself headaches every time you stop and walk away from the truck.
author: @arrowkart4j

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: Girl you being my age is so motivational and so exciting because when we were young I used to watch trucker vlogs all the time wanting to be one, but I’ve given up this as a dream career but man I’m SO happy and excited to see you in this field!!! AMAZING and I’m hoping your channel blows up!!! 🎉
author: @DiamondDeidre

title: A Realistic Day in My Life as a 21 Year Old Truck Driver
body: I'm a female truck mechanic and it's super refreshing to see you in this job. Every single day will have their own bumps in the road and you're doing great! You and your teammate drive safe out there, keep making good decisions <3
author: @honeydaisies

title: Old Dominion University - Full Episode | The College Tour
body: Out of all the great public universities in Virginia--they all have something for everyone--ODU was the best choice for us academically, socially, and price-wise. Go Monarchs!!!
